<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[af:Metaalhaan (astrologie)]][[cdo:Sĭng-iū]][[de:Metall-Hahn]][[hak:Sîn-yû]][[ja:辛酉]][[ko:신유]][[vi:Tân Dậu]][[za:Sinyoux]][[zh-min-nan:Sin-iú]][[zh-yue:辛酉]][[zh:辛酉]]

'''Yin Metal Rooster also known as  Yin Metal Rooster''' ([[Yin Metal Rooster]]) is one of the [[Sexagenary cycle]].

It is the 58th combination in the cycle, preceded by [[Kōshin|Yang Metal Monkey]], and followed by [[Yang Water Dog]]. In the [[Yin-Yang and Wuxing Theory]], the [[Heavenly Stems|Heavenly Stem]] [[Mizunoto (癸)]] represents yin [[Metal (wuxing)|Metal (金)]], and the [[Earthly Branches|Earthly Branch]] [[Rooster (zodiac)|Rooster (雞)]] also represents yin metal, forming a [[Wuxing (Chinese philosophy)#Harmonious Relationship (比和)|Harmonious Relationship (Five Elements)]].

==Pronunciation==
* English: Yin Metal Rooster (辛酉)
** Chinese (Mandarin Pinyin): xīnyǒu
** Cantonese (Jyutping): san1 jau5
**  Japanese:
***Kunyomi 1: Kanoto-tori
***Kunyomi 2: Shinkin-no-tori
***Onyomi 1: shinyuu
** Korean (Revised Romanization, Hangul): sinyu (신유)
** Vietnamese (Hán-Việt): Tân Dậu

==Years of Yin Metal Rooster==
{{Anchors|Xinyou Year|qq=}}
A [[Common Era]] year that leaves a remainder of 1 when divided by 60 is a '''Yin Metal Rooster year'''.

Yin Metal Rooster is considered to have revolutionary significance.

In the ancient Chinese texts known as [[weishu]] (banned by Emperor [[Emperor Yang of Sui|Yang of Sui]]), with surviving fragments found in [[Miyoshi Kiyotsura|Miyoshi Kiyoyuki]]’s “[[kakumei kanmon]]” (collected in ''[[Gunsho Ruijū]]'' vol. 26), it is written that following [[Kōshin|Yang Metal Monkey]], Yin Metal Rooster also embodies the metal element, and Xin represents yin energy, often associated with people becoming cold-hearted. Thus, a Yin Metal Rooster year is considered a year when the [[Mandate of Heaven]] changes, marking revolutionary shifts in [[Dynasty|dynasties]], known as the '''[[Yin Metal Rooster]]'''.

In Japan, era name changes ([[era change]]) during Yin Metal Rooster years were meant to prevent political upheaval. This practice started in Yin Metal Rooster of [[901]] ([[Shōtai]] 4), when scholar-official Miyoshi Kiyoyuki proposed changing the era name to '''[[Engi (era)|Engi]]'''. Frequently, era names were changed consecutively in both [[Kōshin|Yang Metal Monkey]] and Yin Metal Rooster years, a custom that continued until the [[Meiji era|Meiji period]], though such a precedent does not exist in China.

{{Anchors|Xinyou Revolution|qq=}}
During the Meiji era, scholar [[Naka Michiyo]] referenced ''Kakumei Kanbun'' with [[Zheng Xuan]]’s annotation: 
* “The way of Heaven is not distant; after every three and five, it returns. Six cycles ([[Yang Wood Rat]]) form one era, four sixes and two sixes overlap, and three sevens multiply to form 21 cycles, totaling 1,320 years.”
Based on this, it was believed that every '''1,260 years''' (21 cycles × 60 years = 1,260, called ''Ichibu'') there would be a major revolution during a Yin Metal Rooster year. Applying this, Naka theorized that the 9th year of [[Empress Suiko]]’s reign ([[601]]) marked such a revolutionary year, suggesting that Emperor [[Emperor Jimmu|Jimmu]]’s enthronement occurred 1,260 years earlier, in [[660 BC]].

Alternatively, some interpret the annotation as indicating a '''1,320-year cycle''' (60 × 22 = 1,320). In this case, the pivotal year would shift to the year of the [[Yang Wood Rat]], three years after Yin Metal Rooster, considered the '''Jiazi Reform Year''' ([[Yang Wood Rat]]). This places the starting point at [[664]] AD, the year after the [[Battle of Baekgang]], aligning with the legendary founding date of 660 BC.

==Months of Yin Metal Rooster==
A '''Yin Metal Rooster month''' occurs in [[August]] of years ending in 3 or 8 (corresponding to Heavenly Stems [[Mizunoto (癸)]] and [[Tsuchinoe (戊)]]). Depending on the calendar system, this could refer to the [[Japanese Lunisolar Calendar]] month or the solar term period between [[Bailu (solar term)|White Dew]] and the day before [[Hanlu|Cold Dew]].

A '''Yin Metal Rooster day''' is the 7th day in the [[Hassen]] sequence, excluding intercalary days.

{| class="wikitable"
|+'''Yin Metal Rooster Years'''
|-
![[1st millennium BC]]!![[1st millennium|1st millennium AD]]!![[2nd millennium|2nd millennium AD]]!![[3rd millennium|3rd millennium AD]]
|-
|
*[[10th century BC]]
*[[9th century BC]]
*[[840 BC]]
*[[780 BC]]
*[[720 BC]]
*[[660 BC]]
*[[600 BC]]
*[[540 BC]]
*[[480 BC]]
*[[420 BC]]
*[[360 BC]]
*[[300 BC]]
*[[240 BC]]
*[[180 BC]]
*[[120 BC]]
*[[60 BC]]
|
*[[1]]
*[[AD 61|61]]
*[[AD 121|121]]
*[[181]]
*[[241]]
*[[301]]
*[[361]]
*[[421]]
*[[481]]
*[[541]]
*[[601]]
*[[661]]
*[[721]]
*[[781]]
*[[841]]
*[[901]]
*[[961]]
|
*[[1021]]
*[[1081]]
*[[1141]]
*[[1201]]
*[[1261]]
*[[1321]]
*[[1381]]
*[[1441]]
*[[1501]]
*[[1561]]
*[[1621]]
*[[1681]]
*[[1741]]
*[[1801]]
*[[1861]]
*[[1921]]
*[[1981]]
|
*[[2041]]
*[[2101]]
*[[:en:2161|2161]]
*[[2221]]
*[[:en:2281|2281]]
*[[:en:2341|2341]]
*[[2401]]
*[[:en:2461|2461]]
*[[2521]]
*[[2581]]
*[[2641]]
*[[2701]]
*[[2761]]
*[[2821]]
*[[2881]]
*[[:en:2941|2941]]
|}

==Days of Yin Metal Rooster==
* {{Cite book|language=ja |title=[[kakumei kanmon]]: Written by [[Miyoshi no Sukune Kiyoyuki]] |publisher=[[Keizai Zasshisha]] |pages=906–911 |series=[[Gunsho Ruijū]], Vol. 17 |editor=[[Hanawa Hokiichi]] |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1879818/1/458|qq=}}

==See Also==
* [[kakumei kanmon]]
* [[Emperor Jimmu]]
* [[Nihon Shoki]]
* [[Akitake Tsukamoto]]’s ''[[Q11356063]]''

==External Links==
* [https://web.archive.org/web/20110410225801/http://www004.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai1/kakumeikannbunn/gen.htm ''Kakumei Kanbun'' from ''Gunsho Ruijū'', Vol. 26]

{{wikidata link|Q904743}}
[[Category:Chinese astrology]]
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle]]
[[Category:Sexagenary cycle members]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]]
[[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
[[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]
